title: tribuf -formal with a single driver of the tristate net connects input to output
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.35+58 (git sha1 8bd681acf, clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

I have not tested this against git master of yosys as the issue is with the formal tool provided in `oss-cad-suite-linux-x64-20231204` release. The yosys version referenced in the one provided with the release. I can attempt to test on git master if preferred.

- Pull down [this gist](https://gist.github.com/jix/6ccf5b58f10ce77cf40d9f13ed15db86)
- Comment out the instantiation of the `driver_b` instance
- Run `sby -f formal_tristate.sby`
- View the `formal_tristate/model/design.il` and `formal_tristate/model/design_prep.il` files

It is difficult to interpret exact behavior from traces as the input that is tied to `1'bz`  seems to become an `anyseq` variable, from the `design_prep.il` file of the unmodified gist:

```
  cell $pmux $auto$tribuf.cc:165:run$51
    parameter \S_WIDTH 2
    parameter \WIDTH 1
    connect \A \_witness_.anyseq_auto_setundef_cc_533_execute_55
    connect \B { \data_b \data_a }
    connect \S { \driver_b.en \driver_a.en }
    connect \Y \data_bus
  end
```

which I've interpreted as `data_bus` taking on any value at any time when `en == 1'b0`. I'm not certain of this, but I was seeing unexpected behavior in the traces with the enable signals low that could be explained by the above, so I tried to look at the `.il` files instead as they seem to describe the connectivity of the design.

### Expected Behavior

The `design.il` and `design_prep.il` files present connectivity where `bus` is dependent on `en_a` and `data_a`.

Something like the following in the `design_prep.il` file:

```
  cell $pmux $auto$tribuf.cc:165:run$51
    parameter \S_WIDTH 2
    parameter \WIDTH 1
    connect \A \_witness_.anyseq_auto_setundef_cc_533_execute_55
    connect \B { \data_b \data_a }
    connect \S { \driver_b.en \driver_a.en }
    connect \Y \data_bus
  end
...
  connect \bus \data_bus
  connect \driver_a.bus \data_bus
  connect \driver_a.from_bus \data_bus
  connect \driver_a.to_bus \data_a
```

### Actual Behavior

The `data_a` input is connected directly to `bus` and there is no `pmux` cell. From the `design_prep.il` file:

```
  connect \bus \data_a
  connect \data_bus \data_a
  connect \driver_a.bus \data_a
  connect \driver_a.from_bus \data_a
  connect \driver_a.to_bus \data_a
```

    connect \A \_witness_.anyseq_auto_setundef_cc_533_execute_55

    connect \S { \driver_b.en \driver_a.en }

  end

...

  connect \driver_a.from_bus \data_bus
  connect \driver_a.to_bus \data_a
